public int flipLights(int n, int presses) {
    Set<String> states = new HashSet<>();
    
    for (int b1 = 0; b1 <= 1; b1++) {
        for (int b2 = 0; b2 <= 1; b2++) {
            for (int b3 = 0; b3 <= 1; b3++) {
                for (int b4 = 0; b4 <= 1; b4++) {
                    int s = b1 + b2 + b3 + b4;
                    if (s > presses || (s % 2) != (presses % 2)) {
                        continue;
                    }
                    StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
                    for (int j = 1; j <= n; j++) {
                        int flips = 0;
                        if (b1 == 1) flips++;
                        if (b2 == 1 && j % 2 == 0) flips++;
                        if (b3 == 1 && j % 2 == 1) flips++;
                        if (b4 == 1 && (j - 1) % 3 == 0) flips++;
                        sb.append(flips % 2 == 0 ? '1' : '0');
                    }
                    states.add(sb.toString());
                }
            }
        }
    }
    return states.size();
}

Wait, but what about when presses is 0? Then, s must be 0, which is allowed only if b1=b2=b3=b4=0. So, the code correctly adds the state where all bulbs are on.

solution: 
```java
import java.util.HashSet;
import java.util.Set;

class Solution {
    public int flipLights(int n, int presses) {
        Set<String> states = new HashSet<>();
        
        for (int b1 = 0; b1 <= 1; b1++) {
            for (int b2 = 0; b2 <= 1; b2++) {
                for (int b3 = 0; b3 <= 1; b3++) {
                    for (int b4 = 0; b4 <= 1; b4++) {
                        int s = b1 + b2 + b3 + b4;
                        if (s > presses || (s % 2) != (presses % 2)) {
                            continue;
                        }
                        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
                        for (int j = 1; j <= n; j++) {
                            int flips = 0;
                            if (b1 == 1) flips++;
                            if (b2 == 1 && j % 2 == 0) flips++;
                            if (b3 == 1 && j % 2 == 1) flips++;
                            if (b4 == 1 && (j - 1) % 3 == 0) flips++;
                            sb.append(flips % 2 == 0 ? '1' : '0');
                        }
                        states.add(sb.toString());
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return states.size();
    }
}
```

efficiency: HIGH

time complexity: O(16 * n) = O(n)

space complexity: O(n) for storing the state strings.

energy implications: LOW

explanation: The solution efficiently explores all possible parity combinations of button presses, leveraging the fact that each button's effect is determined by whether it's pressed an odd or even number of times. By iterating through all 16 possible combinations and checking their validity based on the total number of presses, the solution computes the resulting bulb states and counts the unique configurations. This approach ensures that we consider all possible states without redundant calculations, making it both time and space efficient.
